{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01PANzCodoASNxyds4bdHPiG","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→\n     5→/**\n     6→ * GET /api/profile/reputation?userId=X\n     7→ *\n     8→ * Returns a user's reputation score and rating breakdown.\n     9→ * If no userId is provided, returns the current user's reputation.\n    10→ */\n    11→export async function GET(req: NextRequest) {\n    12→  const session = await requireAuth();\n    13→  if (!session) {\n    14→    return NextResponse.json(\n    15→      { error: \"Authentication required\" },\n    16→      { status: 401 }\n    17→    );\n    18→  }\n    19→\n    20→  const userIdParam = req.nextUrl.searchParams.get(\"userId\");\n    21→  const userId = userIdParam ? parseInt(userIdParam, 10) : session.userId;\n    22→  const isOwnProfile = userId === session.userId;\n    23→\n    24→  if (userIdParam && isNaN(userId as number)) {\n    25→    return NextResponse.json(\n    26→      { error: \"Invalid userId parameter\" },\n    27→      { status: 400 }\n    28→    );\n    29→  }\n    30→\n    31→  // Average score\n    32→  const stats = db\n    33→    .prepare(\n    34→      `SELECT\n    35→        COUNT(*) as total_ratings,\n    36→        ROUND(AVG(score), 1) as average_score,\n    37→        SUM(CASE WHEN score = 5 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as five_star,\n    38→        SUM(CASE WHEN score = 4 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as four_star,\n    39→        SUM(CASE WHEN score = 3 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as three_star,\n    40→        SUM(CASE WHEN score = 2 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as two_star,\n    41→        SUM(CASE WHEN score = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as one_star\n    42→       FROM ratings\n    43→       WHERE rated_user_id = ?`\n    44→    )\n    45→    .get(userId) as {\n    46→      total_ratings: number;\n    47→      average_score: number | null;\n    48→      five_star: number;\n    49→      four_star: number;\n    50→      three_star: number;\n    51→      two_star: number;\n    52→      one_star: number;\n    53→    };\n    54→\n    55→  // Number of completed groups\n    56→  const completedGroups = db\n    57→    .prepare(\n    58→      `SELECT COUNT(*) as count\n    59→       FROM listing_members lm\n    60→       JOIN listings l ON lm.listing_id = l.id\n    61→       WHERE lm.user_id = ? AND l.is_full = 1`\n    62→    )\n    63→    .get(userId) as { count: number };\n    64→\n    65→  // Number of groups where this user has given ratings\n    66→  const groupsRated = db\n    67→    .prepare(\n    68→      `SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT listing_id) as count\n    69→       FROM ratings\n    70→       WHERE rater_id = ?`\n    71→    )\n    72→    .get(userId) as { count: number };\n    73→\n    74→  // Recent ratings received (with anonymized feedback)\n    75→  const recentRatings = db\n    76→    .prepare(\n    77→      `SELECT r.score, r.comment, r.created_at, l.book_title\n    78→       FROM ratings r\n    79→       JOIN listings l ON r.listing_id = l.id\n    80→       WHERE r.rated_user_id = ?\n    81→       ORDER BY r.created_at DESC\n    82→       LIMIT 10`\n    83→    )\n    84→    .all(userId) as {\n    85→      score: number;\n    86→      comment: string;\n    87→      created_at: string;\n    88→      book_title: string;\n    89→    }[];\n    90→\n    91→  // Strip comments from ratings when viewing other users' profiles (privacy)\n    92→  const sanitizedRatings = isOwnProfile\n    93→    ? recentRatings\n    94→    : recentRatings.map(({ score, created_at, book_title }) => ({\n    95→        score,\n    96→        created_at,\n    97→        book_title,\n    98→      }));\n    99→\n   100→  return NextResponse.json({\n   101→    reputation: {\n   102→      averageScore: stats.average_score || 0,\n   103→      totalRatings: stats.total_ratings,\n   104→      completedGroups: completedGroups.count,\n   105→      groupsRated: groupsRated.count,\n   106→      breakdown: {\n   107→        5: stats.five_star || 0,\n   108→        4: stats.four_star || 0,\n   109→        3: stats.three_star || 0,\n   110→        2: stats.two_star || 0,\n   111→        1: stats.one_star || 0,\n   112→      },\n   113→      recentRatings: sanitizedRatings,\n   114→    },\n   115→  });\n   116→}\n   117→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01JqyyJFaeHKug2tYjd2DZRM","session_id":"bbf58929-c3f8-4e2d-8641-5abb9b9479a7","uuid":"9473ae32-76ca-4541-ac97-ad981a03f23b","timestamp":"2026-03-24T08:02:28.849Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01SmWf7SnGu3q74cGRNmhaxd","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→\n     5→interface ReadingRow {\n     6→  id: number;\n     7→  book_title: string;\n     8→  book_author: string;\n     9→  book_cover_url: string;\n    10→  reading_pace: string;\n    11→  start_date: string;\n    12→  meeting_format: string;\n    13→  is_full: number;\n    14→  telegram_link: string;\n    15→  discord_link: string;\n    16→  member_count: number;\n    17→  max_group_size: number;\n    18→  author_name: string;\n    19→  joined_at: string;\n    20→}\n    21→\n    22→export async function GET() {\n    23→  const session = await requireAuth();\n    24→  if (!session) {\n    25→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n    26→  }\n    27→\n    28→  // Get all listings the user is a member of (including ones they authored)\n    29→  const readings = db.prepare(`\n    30→    SELECT\n    31→      l.id,\n    32→      l.book_title,\n    33→      l.book_author,\n    34→      l.book_cover_url,\n    35→      l.reading_pace,\n    36→      l.start_date,\n    37→      l.meeting_format,\n    38→      l.is_full,\n    39→      l.telegram_link,\n    40→      l.discord_link,\n    41→      l.max_group_size,\n    42→      u.display_name as author_name,\n    43→      lm.joined_at,\n    44→      (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = l.id) as member_count\n    45→    FROM listing_members lm\n    46→    JOIN listings l ON l.id = lm.listing_id\n    47→    JOIN users u ON u.id = l.author_id\n    48→    WHERE lm.user_id = ?\n    49→    ORDER BY lm.joined_at DESC\n    50→  `).all(session.userId) as ReadingRow[];\n    51→\n    52→  const today = new Date().toISOString().split(\"T\")[0];\n    53→\n    54→  // Reading history: completed groups (full + start_date in the past)\n    55→  // Upcoming: not yet started and not full\n    56→  // Active: everything else that has started or is full (but not completed)\n    57→  const isHistory = (r: ReadingRow) => r.is_full === 1 && r.start_date < today;\n    58→  const isUpcoming = (r: ReadingRow) => r.is_full === 0 && r.start_date > today;\n    59→\n    60→  const history = readings.filter(isHistory);\n    61→  const upcoming = readings.filter(isUpcoming);\n    62→  const active = readings.filter(\n    63→    (r) => !isHistory(r) && !isUpcoming(r) && (r.is_full === 1 || r.start_date <= today)\n    64→  );\n    65→\n    66→  return NextResponse.json({\n    67→    active,\n    68→    history,\n    69→    upcoming,\n    70→    total: readings.length,\n    71→  });\n    72→}\n    73→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01JqyyJFaeHKug2tYjd2DZRM","session_id":"bbf58929-c3f8-4e2d-8641-5abb9b9479a7","uuid":"4de8a854-419c-4b5c-b4cc-e1a4cdab5041","timestamp":"2026-03-24T08:02:29.102Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01Xd7exXiYXsi7R5Wh3vNLbM","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { getIronSession, type SessionOptions } from \"iron-session\";\n     2→import { cookies } from \"next/headers\";\n     3→\n     4→export interface SessionData {\n     5→  userId?: number;\n     6→  email?: string;\n     7→  displayName?: string;\n     8→}\n     9→\n    10→const DEV_SECRET =\n    11→  \"bookmate-development-secret-key-change-in-production-32chars!\";\n    12→\n    13→function getSessionOptions(): SessionOptions {\n    14→  const secret = process.env.SESSION_SECRET;\n    15→  if (!secret && process.env.NODE_ENV === \"production\") {\n    16→    throw new Error(\n    17→      \"SESSION_SECRET environment variable is required in production\"\n    18→    );\n    19→  }\n    20→  return {\n    21→    password: secret || DEV_SECRET,\n    22→    cookieName: \"bookmate_session\",\n    23→    cookieOptions: {\n    24→      httpOnly: true,\n    25→      secure: process.env.NODE_ENV === \"production\" && process.env.HTTPS_ENABLED === \"true\",\n    26→      sameSite: \"lax\",\n    27→      maxAge: 60 * 60 * 24 * 7, // 1 week\n    28→    },\n    29→  };\n    30→}\n    31→\n    32→export async function getSession() {\n    33→  const cookieStore = await cookies();\n    34→  return getIronSession<SessionData>(cookieStore, getSessionOptions());\n    35→}\n    36→\n    37→export async function requireAuth() {\n    38→  const session = await getSession();\n    39→  if (!session.userId) {\n    40→    return null;\n    41→  }\n    42→  return session;\n    43→}\n    44→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01JqyyJFaeHKug2tYjd2DZRM","session_id":"bbf58929-c3f8-4e2d-8641-5abb9b9479a7","uuid":"0f0ea783-bc34-4175-bfaa-65920a5566d6","timestamp":"2026-03-24T08:02:32.248Z"}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01WjYt48Urr3rbUftsE2Uubt","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→\"use client\";\n     2→\n     3→import { useState, useEffect } from \"react\";\n     4→import { useParams, useRouter } from \"next/navigation\";\n     5→import Image from \"next/image\";\n     6→\n     7→export default function EditListingPage() {\n     8→  const { id } = useParams();\n     9→  const router = useRouter();\n    10→  const [error, setError] = useState(\"\");\n    11→  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(false);\n    12→  const [fetching, setFetching] = useState(true);\n    13→  const [fetchFailed, setFetchFailed] = useState(false);\n    14→\n    15→  const [bookTitle, setBookTitle] = useState(\"\");\n    16→  const [bookAuthor, setBookAuthor] = useState(\"\");\n    17→  const [bookCoverUrl, setBookCoverUrl] = useState(\"\");\n    18→  const [language, setLanguage] = useState(\"English\");\n    19→  const [readingPace, setReadingPace] = useState(\"\");\n    20→  const [startDate, setStartDate] = useState(\"\");\n    21→  const [meetingFormat, setMeetingFormat] = useState(\"text\");\n    22→  const [maxGroupSize, setMaxGroupSize] = useState(\"4\");\n    23→  const [requiresApproval, setRequiresApproval] = useState(false);\n    24→  const [platformPreference, setPlatformPreference] = useState(\"telegram\");\n    25→  const [memberCount, setMemberCount] = useState(1);\n    26→\n    27→  useEffect(() => {\n    28→    async function fetchListing() {\n    29→      try {\n    30→        const res = await fetch(`/api/listings/${id}`);\n    31→        const data = await res.json();\n    32→        if (res.ok && data.listing) {\n    33→          const l = data.listing;\n    34→          if (!l.isAuthor) {\n    35→            router.push(`/listings/${id}`);\n    36→            return;\n    37→          }\n    38→          // Cannot edit if platform link shared\n    39→          if (l.telegram_link || l.discord_link) {\n    40→            router.push(`/listings/${id}`);\n    41→            return;\n    42→          }\n    43→          setBookTitle(l.book_title);\n    44→          setBookAuthor(l.book_author);\n    45→          setBookCoverUrl(l.book_cover_url || \"\");\n    46→          setLanguage(l.language || \"English\");\n    47→          setReadingPace(l.reading_pace || {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01DLReDPgyUBhLjvnQb3NuPf","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→\n     5→export async function POST(\n     6→  req: NextRequest,\n     7→  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n     8→) {\n     9→  const session = await requireAuth();\n    10→  if (!session) {\n    11→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n    12→  }\n    13→\n    14→  const { id } = await params;\n    15→  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n    16→  if (isNaN(listingId)) {\n    17→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid listing ID\" }, { status: 400 });\n    18→  }\n    19→\n    20→  const listing = db\n    21→    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    22→    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n    23→\n    24→  if (!listing) {\n    25→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n    26→  }\n    27→\n    28→  if (listing.author_id !== session.userId) {\n    29→    return NextResponse.json(\n    30→      { error: \"Only the listing author can set the Telegram link\" },\n    31→      { status: 403 }\n    32→    );\n    33→  }\n    34→\n    35→  if (!listing.is_full) {\n    36→    return NextResponse.json(\n    37→      { error: \"Group must be full before sharing a platform link\" },\n    38→      { status: 400 }\n    39→    );\n    40→  }\n    41→\n    42→  const { telegramLink } = await req.json();\n    43→\n    44→  if (!telegramLink || !telegramLink.startsWith(\"https://t.me/\") || telegramLink.length > 512) {\n    45→    return NextResponse.json(\n    46→      { error: \"Please provide a valid Telegram invite link (https://t.me/..., max 512 characters)\" },\n    47→      { status: 400 }\n    48→    );\n    49→  }\n    50→\n    51→  db.prepare(\"UPDATE listings SET telegram_link = ? WHERE id = ?\").run(\n    52→    telegramLink.trim(),\n    53→    listingId\n    54→  );\n    55→\n    56→  return NextResponse.json({ ok: true });\n    57→}\n    58→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01JqyyJFaeHKug2tYjd2DZRM","session_id":"bbf58929-c3f8-4e2d-8641-5abb9b9479a7","uuid":"2716be08-29a2-4313-8cc7-87924fae3b7f","timestamp":"2026-03-24T08:02:34.996Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01Qk1BndAbUn7LjwiM4G1XxP","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→\"use client\";\n     2→\n     3→import { useEffect, useState } from \"react\";\n     4→import Link from \"next/link\";\n     5→\n     6→interface Notification {\n     7→  id: number;\n     8→  listing_id: number;\n     9→  type: string;\n    10→  message: string;\n    11→  is_read: number;\n    12→  created_at: string;\n    13→  book_title: string;\n    14→}\n    15→\n    16→export default function NotificationsPage() {\n    17→  const [notifications, setNotifications] = useState<Notification[]>([]);\n    18→  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true);\n    19→  const [error, setError] = useState<string | null>(null);\n    20→\n    21→  useEffect(() => {\n    22→    fetch(\"/api/notifications\")\n    23→      .then((r) => {\n    24→        if (!r.ok) throw new Error(\"Failed to load notifications\");\n    25→        return r.json();\n    26→      })\n    27→      .then((data) => {\n    28→        setNotifications(data.notifications || []);\n    29→        // Mark all as read (fire-and-forget with silent catch)\n    30→        fetch(\"/api/notifications\", {\n    31→          method: \"PATCH\",\n    32→          headers: { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\" },\n    33→          body: JSON.stringify({}),\n    34→        }).catch(() => {});\n    35→      })\n    36→      .catch(() => {\n    37→        setError(\"Could not load notifications. Please try again.\");\n    38→      })\n    39→      .finally(() => {\n    40→        setLoading(false);\n    41→      });\n    42→  }, []);\n    43→\n    44→  if (loading) {\n    45→    return (\n    46→      <div className=\"text-center py-16\" style={{ color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\" }}>\n    47→        <p style={{ fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}>Loading...</p>\n    48→      </div>\n    49→    );\n    50→  }\n    51→\n    52→  if (error) {\n    53→    return (\n    54→      <div className=\"max-w-2xl mx-auto\">\n    55→        <h1 className=\"text-2xl sm:text-3xl mb-4 sm:mb-6\">Notifications</h1>\n    56→        <div className=\"text-center py-12\">\n    57→          <p\n    58→            className=\"text-sm mb-4\"\n    59→            style={{ color: \"var(--color-error)\", fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}\n    60→            role=\"alert\"\n    61→          >\n    62→            {error}\n    63→          </p>\n    64→          <button\n    65→            onClick={() => window.location.reload()}\n    66→            className=\"btn-primary\"\n    67→          >\n    68→            Try again\n    69→          </button>\n    70→        </div>\n    71→      </div>\n    72→    );\n    73→  }\n    74→\n    75→  return (\n    76→    <div className=\"max-w-2xl mx-auto\">\n    77→      <h1 className=\"text-2xl sm:text-3xl mb-4 sm:mb-6\">Notifications</h1>\n    78→\n    79→      {notifications.length === 0 ? (\n    80→        <div className=\"text-center py-12\">\n    81→          <p style={{ color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\", fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}>\n    82→            No notifications yet\n    83→          </p>\n    84→        </div>\n    85→      ) : (\n    86→        <div className=\"space-y-2\">\n    87→          {notifications.map((n) => (\n    88→            <Link\n    89→              key={n.id}\n    90→              href={n.listing_id ? `/listings/${n.listing_id}` : \"#\"}\n    91→              className=\"card block hover:shadow-sm transition-shadow\"\n    92→              style={{\n    93→                borderLeftWidth: \"3px\",\n    94→                borderLeftColor: n.is_read\n    95→                  ? \"var(--color-border)\"\n    96→                  : \"var(--color-accent)\",\n    97→              }}\n    98→              aria-label={`${!n.is_read ? \"Unread: \" : \"\"}${n.message}`}\n    99→            >\n   100→              {!n.is_read && (\n   101→                <span className=\"sr-only\">Unread</span>\n   102→              )}\n   103→              <p\n   104→                className=\"text-sm\"\n   105→                style={{ fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}\n   106→              >\n   107→                {n.message}\n   108→              </p>\n   109→              <p\n   110→                className=\"text-xs mt-1\"\n   111→                style={{\n   112→                  color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\",\n   113→                  fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\",\n   114→                }}\n   115→              >\n   116→                {new Date(n.created_at).toLocaleDateString(\"en-US\", {\n   117→                  month: \"short\",\n   118→                  day: \"numeric\",\n   119→                  hour: \"numeric\",\n   120→                  minute: \"2-digit\",\n   121→                })}\n   122→              </p>\n   123→            </Link>\n   124→          ))}\n   125→        </div>\n   126→      )}\n   127→    </div>\n   128→  );\n   129→}\n   130→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01JqyyJFaeHKug2tYjd2DZRM","session_id":"bbf58929-c3f8-4e2d-8641-5abb9b9479a7","uuid":"6cfbcdee-4a9d-4374-9b0d-76114c79389c","timestamp":"2026-03-24T08:02:35.484Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01UQemWqyYwqUBCWmEfeMWvR","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { getSession } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→import { isBotConfigured, generateBotInviteUrl } from \"@/lib/discord\";\n     5→\n     6→export async function GET(\n     7→  _req: NextRequest,\n     8→  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n     9→) {\n    10→  const session = await getSession();\n    11→  if (!session.userId) {\n    12→    return NextResponse.json(\n    13→      { error: \"Authentication required\" },\n    14→      { status: 401 }\n    15→    );\n    16→  }\n    17→\n    18→  if (!isBotConfigured()) {\n    19→    return NextResponse.json(\n    20→      { error: \"Discord bot is not configured\" },\n    21→      { status: 503 }\n    22→    );\n    23→  }\n    24→\n    25→  const { id } = await params;\n    26→  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n    27→  if (isNaN(listingId)) {\n    28→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid listing ID\" }, { status: 400 });\n    29→  }\n    30→\n    31→  const listing = db\n    32→    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    33→    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n    34→\n    35→  if (!listing) {\n    36→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n    37→  }\n    38→\n    39→  if (listing.author_id !== session.userId) {\n    40→    return NextResponse.json(\n    41→      { error: \"Only the listing author can set up the Discord group\" },\n    42→      { status: 403 }\n    43→    );\n    44→  }\n    45→\n    46→  if (!listing.is_full) {\n    47→    return NextResponse.json(\n    48→      { error: \"The group is not full yet\" },\n    49→      { status: 400 }\n    50→    );\n    51→  }\n    52→\n    53→  if (listing.discord_link) {\n    54→    return NextResponse.json(\n    55→      { error: \"A Discord link has already been set\" },\n    56→      { status: 400 }\n    57→    );\n    58→  }\n    59→\n    60→  const inviteUrl = generateBotInviteUrl(listingId);\n    61→\n    62→  return NextResponse.json({\n    63→    inviteUrl,\n    64→    instructions:\n    65→      \"Click the link to add the Bookmate bot to your Discord server. \" +\n    66→      \"The bot will create a reading group channel and generate an invite link for your members.\",\n    67→  });\n    68→}\n    69→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01JqyyJFaeHKug2tYjd2DZRM","session_id":"bbf58929-c3f8-4e2d-8641-5abb9b9479a7","uuid":"5879f3fd-ddb1-489a-9702-5c722a3c02ea","timestamp":"2026-03-24T08:02:40.236Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01YX5oYdJFQzYLgqr5VoZxvn","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→/**\n     2→ * Telegram Bot API integration for automatic group creation.\n     3→ *\n     4→ * When TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN is set, the system can:\n     5→ * 1. Generate deep links for one-click group creation\n     6→ * 2. Detect when the bot is added to a group (via webhook)\n     7→ * 3. Auto-generate and save invite links\n     8→ *\n     9→ * Flow:\n    10→ * - Author clicks \"Create Telegram Group\" → opens deep link in Telegram\n    11→ * - Telegram prompts author to create a group with the bot\n    12→ * - Bot receives `my_chat_member` update via webhook\n    13→ * - Bot creates an invite link and saves it to the listing\n    14→ */\n    15→\n    16→const BOT_TOKEN = process.env.TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN || \"\";\n    17→const TELEGRAM_API = `https://api.telegram.org/bot${BOT_TOKEN}`;\n    18→\n    19→export function isBotConfigured(): boolean {\n    20→  return BOT_TOKEN.length > 0;\n    21→}\n    22→\n    23→/**\n    24→ * Call the Telegram Bot API.\n    25→ */\n    26→async function callApi<T = unknown>(\n    27→  method: string,\n    28→  params?: Record<string, unknown>\n    29→): Promise<{ ok: boolean; result?: T; description?: string }> {\n    30→  const res = await fetch(`${TELEGRAM_API}/${method}`, {\n    31→    method: \"POST\",\n    32→    headers: { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\" },\n    33→    body: JSON.stringify(params || {}),\n    34→  });\n    35→  return res.json();\n    36→}\n    37→\n    38→/**\n    39→ * Get bot info (username, id).\n    40→ */\n    41→export async function getBotInfo(): Promise<{\n    42→  id: number;\n    43→  username: string;\n    44→  first_name: string;\n    45→} | null> {\n    46→  const res = await callApi<{\n    47→    id: number;\n    48→    username: string;\n    49→    first_name: string;\n    50→  }>(\"getMe\");\n    51→  return res.ok ? res.result! : null;\n    52→}\n    53→\n    54→/**\n    55→ * Generate a deep link that opens Telegram and prompts the user\n    56→ * to create a new group with the bot already added.\n    57→ *\n    58→ * Format: https://t.me/BOT_USERNAME?startgroup=listing_LISTING_ID\n    59→ *\n    60→ * When the user creates the group, Telegram adds the bot and\n    61→ * sends a `my_chat_member` update to our webhook.\n    62→ */\n    63→export async function generateGroupDeepLink(\n    64→  listingId: number\n    65→): Promise<string | null> {\n    66→  const bot = await getBotInfo();\n    67→  if (!bot) return null;\n    68→  return `https://t.me/${bot.username}?startgroup=listing_${listingId}`;\n    69→}\n    70→\n    71→/**\n    72→ * Create an invite link for a chat where the bot is an admin.\n    73→ */\n    74→export async function createChatInviteLink(\n    75→  chatId: number | string\n    76→): Promise<string | null> {\n    77→  const res = await callApi<{ invite_link: string }>(\n    78→    \"createChatInviteLink\",\n    79→    {\n    80→      chat_id: chatId,"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01JqyyJFaeHKug2tYjd2DZRM","session_id":"bbf58929-c3f8-4e2d-8641-5abb9b9479a7","uuid":"474eff4b-cb79-4624-bcfb-c80226d11e74","timestamp":"2026-03-24T08:02:40.430Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01W9d3RbH6N8FUxmXEhv2dV9","type":"tool_result","content":"__tests__/api/listings/listing-join.test.ts:90:  notifyGroupFull: vi.fn(),\n__tests__/api/listings/listing-join.test.ts:254:    const { notifyListingAuthor, notifyGroupFull } = await import(\"@/lib/notifications\");\n__tests__/api/listings/listing-join.test.ts:262:    expect(notifyGroupFull).not.toHaveBeenCalled();\n__tests__/api/listings/listing-join.test.ts:290:  it(\"calls notifyGroupFull when group becomes full\", async () => {\n__tests__/api/listings/listing-join.test.ts:291:    const { notifyGroupFull } = await import(\"@/lib/notifications\");\n__tests__/api/listings/listing-join.test.ts:298:    expect(notifyGroupFull).toHaveBeenCalledWith(listingId);\nlib/notifications.ts:121:export function notifyGroupFull(listingId: number) {\napp/api/listings/[id]/applications/route.ts:4:import { notifyApplicationDecision, notifyGroupFull } from \"@/lib/notifications\";\napp/api/listings/[id]/applications/route.ts:113:          notifyGroupFull(listingId);\napp/api/listings/[id]/join/route.ts:4:import { notifyListingAuthor, notifyGroupFull } from \"@/lib/notifications\";\napp/api/listings/[id]/join/route.ts:111:    notifyGroupFull(listingId);"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01JqyyJFaeHKug2tYjd2DZRM","session_id":"bbf58929-c3f8-4e2d-8641-5abb9b9479a7","uuid":"cf8fd0bb-3e14-4a7b-b476-b7fc5a53ff24","timestamp":"2026-03-24T08:02:44.116Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01LYtsSsFdkyhW9Ld6DwAUvo","type":"tool_result","content":"    59→  if (action === \"approve\") {\n    60→    // Use transaction to ensure atomicity\n    61→    const approveTransaction = db.transaction(() => {\n    62→      // Check if group is already full\n    63→      const { count } = db\n    64→        .prepare(\"SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\")\n    65→        .get(listingId) as { count: number };\n    66→\n    67→      if (count >= (listing.max_group_size as number)) {\n    68→        throw new Error(\"Group is already full\");\n    69→      }\n    70→\n    71→      // Update application status\n    72→      db.prepare(\n    73→        \"UPDATE listing_applications SET status = 'approved', decided_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ?\"\n    74→      ).run(applicationId);\n    75→\n    76→      // Add to members\n    77→      db.prepare(\n    78→        \"INSERT INTO listing_members (listing_id, user_id) VALUES (?, ?)\"\n    79→      ).run(listingId, applicantUserId);\n    80→\n    81→      // Check if group is now full\n    82→      const newCount = count + 1;\n    83→      let rejectedApps: { id: number; user_id: number }[] = [];\n    84→      if (newCount >= (listing.max_group_size as number)) {\n    85→        db.prepare(\"UPDATE listings SET is_full = 1 WHERE id = ?\").run(listingId);\n    86→\n    87→        // Reject remaining pending applications inside the transaction\n    88→        rejectedApps = db\n    89→          .prepare(\n    90→            \"SELECT id, user_id FROM listing_applications WHERE listing_id = ? AND status = 'pending'\"\n    91→          )\n    92→          .all(listingId) as { id: number; user_id: number }[];\n    93→\n    94→        for (const app of rejectedApps) {\n    95→          db.prepare(\n    96→            \"UPDATE listing_applications SET status = 'rejected', decided_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ?\"\n    97→          ).run(app.id);\n    98→        }\n    99→\n   100→        return { isFull: true, rejectedApps };\n   101→      }\n   102→      return { isFull: false, rejectedApps };\n   103→    });"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01JqyyJFaeHKug2tYjd2DZRM","session_id":"bbf58929-c3f8-4e2d-8641-5abb9b9479a7","uuid":"8d0f05dd-7366-4a20-a35e-936f9dce934d","timestamp":"2026-03-24T08:02:47.623Z"}
{"type":"system","subtype":"task_notification","task_id":"a7169b9fc38a372a7","tool_use_id":"toolu_01JqyyJFaeHKug2tYjd2DZRM","status":"completed","output_file":"","summary":"Code review round 11","usage":{"total_tokens":78569,"tool_uses":33,"duration_ms":108680},"uuid":"8c12fc09-a869-49b0-9628-9710752e6e2a","session_id":"bbf58929-c3f8-4e2d-8641-5abb9b9479a7"}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01JqyyJFaeHKug2tYjd2DZRM","type":"tool_result","content":[{"type":"text","text":"I now have a thorough understanding of the codebase. Let me compile my findings.\n\n---\n\n## Code Review — Round 11\n\nReviewing the Bookmate codebase at `/root/Projects/bookmate/app` with focus on data integrity, business logic edge cases, resource leaks, client-side state bugs, and build/deploy issues.\n\n---\n\n## Critical Issues\n\n### 1. Stale `listing.max_group_size` used inside transaction in applications route\n\n**Confidence: 95**\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/applications/route.ts`, lines 67 and 84\n\nThe `listing` object is fetched *outside* the transaction at line 21 (`SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?`). The `approveTransaction` callback then closes over this stale snapshot via `listing.max_group_size`. Between the outer fetch and the transaction executing, a concurrent PATCH to the listing could have changed `max_group_size`. The join route correctly re-reads `max_group_size` inside its own transaction (`SELECT max_group_size FROM listings WHERE id = ?`), but the applications route does not.\n\n**Impact:** The organizer could set `max_group_size = 3` concurrently with an approval, and the guard on line 67 would compare against the old value, allowing one extra member to be added.\n\n**Fix:** Inside `approveTransaction`, re-read `max_group_size` from the DB:\n\n```ts\nconst freshListing = db\n  .prepare(\"SELECT max_group_size FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n  .get(listingId) as { max_group_size: number } | undefined;\nif (!freshListing) throw new Error(\"Listing not found\");\nconst maxSize = freshListing.max_group_size;\n// use maxSize instead of (listing.max_group_size as number) on lines 67 and 84\n```\n\n---\n\n### 2. Missing CASCADE constraints — deleting a user leaves orphaned rows\n\n**Confidence: 92**\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/db.ts`, lines 45, 52–55, 58–62, 65–74, 76–88, and second `db.exec` block\n\nThere is no user account deletion endpoint, but the schema's FK definitions (`REFERENCES users(id)`) have no `ON DELETE CASCADE`. If a user were ever manually deleted from the DB, or if a deletion endpoint is added later, rows in `listing_members`, `user_genres`, `notifications`, `listing_applications`, `ratings`, `telegram_user_links`, and the `listings` table (with its cascade of dependents) would be left orphaned. SQLite with `foreign_keys = ON` would instead **block** the delete entirely, which is arguably the safer failure mode — but listings whose `author_id` no longer exists would become permanently inaccessible: the GET `/api/listings/[id]` JOIN on `users` would silently return no rows (`undefined`), showing a 404 to all members.\n\n**Impact:** Any future account-deletion feature, or a manual data-repair operation, would either be blocked silently or silently orphan data.\n\n**Fix:** Add `ON DELETE CASCADE` to child-table FKs where orphan rows have no standalone value (e.g., `listing_members`, `user_genres`, `notifications`, `listing_applications`, `ratings`, `telegram_user_links`). Keep `listings.author_id` as `ON DELETE RESTRICT` or handle deletion explicitly with a dedicated transaction.\n\n---\n\n## Important Issues\n\n### 3. `notifyGroupFull` sends notifications to the group author even when the author is not in `listing_members`\n\n**Confidence: 85**\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/notifications.ts`, lines 127–148\n\n`notifyGroupFull` queries `SELECT user_id FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?` (line 127) and notifies every row. The author is auto-joined as a member at creation time (`create/route.ts` line 134), so this works in the normal case. However, nothing in the DB schema or application code prevents a hypothetical scenario where the author's membership row is removed. More concretely, the comment on line 125 says \"Include all members **(including the author)**\" — if the author row were ever missing, they would not receive the \"group is full\" notification and would never know to create the platform chat, silently breaking the post-full flow.\n\nThis is low-severity today because the author is always auto-joined and there is no leave-group endpoint, but the fragile dependency on that invariant is worth noting.\n\n**Confidence: 85** — the author is always in `listing_members` today, so this is not a current bug but a latent one if a leave-group feature is ever added.\n\n---\n\n### 4. `reading_pace` filter in listings API performs a LIKE match against free-text user input\n\n**Confidence: 83**\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/route.ts`, lines 46–49\n\n```ts\nif (readingPace && readingPace.length <= 200) {\n  conditions.push(\"l.reading_pace LIKE ?\");\n  params.push(`%${readingPace}%`);\n}\n```\n\nThe `readingPace` filter is a substring LIKE over an open-ended text field. The UI only offers four preset values (`chapter`, `pages`, `week`, `day`) — but the API accepts any string up to 200 characters. A request with `reading_pace=a` would match a large fraction of listings (\"daily\", \"chapter\", etc.) and return more results than the user expects. More importantly, `reading_pace` is a free-text field stored from listing creation (e.g., \"1 chapter per week\"), so the UI filter values are only matching substrings of that text by coincidence. If a user creates a listing with `reading_pace = \"daily pages\"`, it would match both the `pages` filter and the `day` filter simultaneously.\n\nThis is a data-model/logic consistency bug: the filter assumes `reading_pace` stores a tag/enum but the create form stores an arbitrary user-entered string. The count and totalPages shown to the user would be misleading for any search involving reading pace.\n\n---\n\n### 5. `debounceRef` and `abortRef` in `HomePage` are not cleaned up on unmount\n\n**Confidence: 82**\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/page.tsx`, lines 42–44, 95–98\n\n```ts\nconst debounceRef = useRef<ReturnType<typeof setTimeout>>(undefined);\nconst abortRef = useRef<AbortController>(undefined);\n```\n\n`fetchListings` aborts any in-flight request when a new one starts (line 48), but there is no `useEffect` cleanup that aborts the pending request or clears the debounce timer when the component unmounts. If a user navigates away during a debounced search or while a fetch is in-flight, the timeout callback will fire and call `fetch()` after the component unmounts. This will throw an `AbortError` (since the controller's signal is still pending), trigger the `.catch()` handler, and try to call `setLoading(false)` and `setError(...)` on an unmounted component.\n\nIn React 18 this no longer throws a warning (the \"setState on unmounted component\" warning was removed), but the orphaned fetch and timer still consume resources and could cause subtle bugs if the component is ever remounted within the debounce window.\n\n**Fix:** Add a cleanup effect:\n\n```ts\nuseEffect(() => {\n  return () => {\n    if (debounceRef.current) clearTimeout(debounceRef.current);\n    if (abortRef.current) abortRef.current.abort();\n  };\n}, []);\n```\n\n---\n\n### 6. `notifyGroupFull` is called *after* notifying the author join — double notification to members who just joined\n\n**Confidence: 80**\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/join/route.ts`, lines 108–112\n\n```ts\nnotifyListingAuthor(listingId, session.displayName || \"Someone\");\n\nif (result.count === result.maxSize) {\n  notifyGroupFull(listingId);\n}\n```\n\n`notifyListingAuthor` creates a `new_member` notification for the author. `notifyGroupFull` (called immediately after when the group just filled) then notifies *all members* including the author again. The author therefore receives two notifications in rapid succession when they are the last person to see someone join: one `new_member` and one `group_full`. This is functionally acceptable, but the `group_full` notification sent to the new joiner (the person who just joined) is also sent to the author via `notifyGroupFull`'s loop over `listing_members`, which includes the author. So the author gets both.\n\nMore concretely: the author is a member (auto-joined), so `notifyGroupFull` includes them. This means the author always gets a `group_full` notification in addition to the `new_member` notification. Two emails fire for a single join event.\n\nThis is a UX/logic issue rather than a security or data-integrity bug, but it will be noticeable every time a group fills.\n\n---\n\n## Summary\n\n| # | Severity | Issue | File |\n|---|----------|-------|------|\n| 1 | Critical | Stale `max_group_size` read inside `approveTransaction` | `/app/api/listings/[id]/applications/route.ts:67,84` |\n| 2 | Critical | No `ON DELETE CASCADE` — user deletion orphans or blocks all related data | `/lib/db.ts` (schema) |\n| 3 | Important | `notifyGroupFull` relies on author being in `listing_members` with no schema guarantee | `/lib/notifications.ts:127-128` |\n| 4 | Important | `reading_pace` LIKE filter vs free-text field causes misleading pagination counts | `/app/api/listings/route.ts:46-49` |\n| 5 | Important | Missing `useEffect` cleanup for debounce timer and AbortController on unmount | `/app/page.tsx:42-44,95-98` |\n| 6 | Important | Author receives duplicate notifications (new_member + group_full) when group fills | `/app/api/listings/[id]/join/route.ts:108-112` |"},{"type":"text","text":"agentId: a7169b9fc38a372a7 (use SendMessage with to: 'a7169b9fc38a372a7' to continue this agent)\n<usage>total_tokens: 80029\ntool_uses: 33\nduration_ms: 108682</usage>"}]}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"bbf58929-c3f8-4e2d-8641-5abb9b9479a7","uuid":"6df3219d-2f66-47be-a520-9369211377c5","timestamp":"2026-03-24T08:03:39.448Z","tool_use_result":{"status":"completed","prompt":"Review the Bookmate codebase at /root/Projects/bookmate/app for bugs, logic errors, security vulnerabilities, and code quality issues. \n\nThis is code review round 11. Previous rounds (1-10) already covered:\n- API route security (auth checks, input validation, rate limiting)\n- SQLite race conditions and transaction safety\n- Telegram/Discord webhook validation and HTML injection\n- Client-side race conditions (AbortController on fetches)\n- Date timezone bugs\n- Notification FK violations\n- Stale data in transactions\n- Timing-safe secret comparison\n- Type validation on JSON body fields\n- Email HTML escaping\n- ARIA accessibility\n- Error boundaries and loading states\n- Book cover URL validation\n- Cookie security (httpOnly, secure)\n\nFocus on areas that are LESS LIKELY to have been covered:\n1. **Data integrity**: Are there any cases where data could become inconsistent across tables? Missing CASCADE or orphaned records?\n2. **Edge cases in business logic**: What happens when listings are edited while someone is joining? When a user deletes their account? When the DB file is corrupted?\n3. **Memory leaks or resource leaks**: Any unclosed DB connections, event listeners not cleaned up, intervals not cleared?\n4. **Client-side state management**: Are there any stale closures, stale refs, or component state bugs?\n5. **Build/deploy issues**: Any issues with the build configuration, environment variables, or deployment setup?\n\nOnly report HIGH-CONFIDENCE bugs with clear impact. Do not report style preferences, theoretical concerns, or issues that have already been fixed. Include file paths and line numbers.","agentId":"a7169b9fc38a372a7","content":[{"type":"text","text":"I now have a thorough understanding of the codebase. Let me compile my findings.\n\n---\n\n## Code Review — Round 11\n\nReviewing the Bookmate codebase at `/root/Projects/bookmate/app` with focus on data integrity, business logic edge cases, resource leaks, client-side state bugs, and build/deploy issues.\n\n---\n\n## Critical Issues\n\n### 1. Stale `listing.max_group_size` used inside transaction in applications route\n\n**Confidence: 95**\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/applications/route.ts`, lines 67 and 84\n\nThe `listing` object is fetched *outside* the transaction at line 21 (`SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?`). Between the outer fetch and the transaction executing, a concurrent PATCH to the listing could have changed `max_group_size`. The join route correctly re-reads `max_group_size` inside its own transaction (`SELECT max_group_size FROM listings WHERE id = ?`), but the applications route does not.\n\n**Impact:** The organizer could set `max_group_size = 3` concurrently with an approval, and the guard on line 67 would compare against the old value, allowing one extra member to be added.\n\n**Fix:** Inside `approveTransaction`, re-read `max_group_size` from the DB:\n\n```ts\nconst freshListing = db\n  .prepare(\"SELECT max_group_size FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n  .get(listingId) as { max_group_size: number } | undefined;\nif (!freshListing) throw new Error(\"Listing not found\");\nconst maxSize = freshListing.max_group_size;\n// use maxSize instead of (listing.max_group_size as number) on lines 67 and 84\n```\n\n---\n\n### 2. Missing CASCADE constraints — deleting a user leaves orphaned rows\n\n**Confidence: 92**\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/db.ts`, lines 45, 52–55, 58–62, 65–74, 76–88, and second `db.exec` block\n\nThere is no user account deletion endpoint, but the schema's FK definitions (`REFERENCES users(id)`) have no `ON DELETE CASCADE`. If a user were ever manually deleted from the DB, or if a deletion endpoint is added later, rows in `listing_members`, `user_genres`, `notifications`, `listing_applications`, `ratings`, `telegram_user_links`, and the `listings` table (with its cascade of dependents) would be left orphaned. SQLite with `foreign_keys = ON` would instead **block** the delete entirely, which is arguably the safer failure mode — but listings whose `author_id` no longer exists would become permanently inaccessible: the GET `/api/listings/[id]` JOIN on `users` would silently return no rows (`undefined`), showing a 404 to all members.\n\n**Impact:** Any future account-deletion feature, or a manual data-repair operation, would either be blocked silently or silently orphan data.\n\n**Fix:** Add `ON DELETE CASCADE` to child-table FKs where orphan rows have no standalone value (e.g., `listing_members`, `user_genres`, `notifications`, `listing_applications`, `ratings`, `telegram_user_links`). Keep `listings.author_id` as `ON DELETE RESTRICT` or handle deletion explicitly with a dedicated transaction.\n\n---\n\n## Important Issues\n\n### 3. `notifyGroupFull` sends notifications to the group author even when the author is not in `listing_members`\n\n**Confidence: 85**\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/notifications.ts`, lines 127–148\n\n`notifyGroupFull` queries `SELECT user_id FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?` (line 127) and notifies every row. The author is auto-joined as a member at creation time (`create/route.ts` line 134), so this works in the normal case. However, nothing in the DB schema or application code prevents a hypothetical scenario where the author's membership row is removed. More concretely, the comment on line 125 says \"Include all members **(including the author)**\" — if the author row were ever missing, they would not receive the \"group is full\" notification and would never know to create the platform chat, silently breaking the post-full flow.\n\nThis is low-severity today because the author is always auto-joined and there is no leave-group endpoint, but the fragile dependency on that invariant is worth noting.\n\n**Confidence: 85** — the author is always in `listing_members` today, so this is not a current bug but a latent one if a leave-group feature is ever added.\n\n---\n\n### 4. `reading_pace` filter in listings API performs a LIKE match against free-text user input\n\n**Confidence: 83**\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/route.ts`, lines 46–49\n\n```ts\nif (readingPace && readingPace.length <= 200) {\n  conditions.push(\"l.reading_pace LIKE ?\");\n  params.push(`%${readingPace}%`);\n}\n```\n\nThe `readingPace` filter is a substring LIKE over an open-ended text field. The UI only offers four preset values (`chapter`, `pages`, `week`, `day`) — but the API accepts any string up to 200 characters. A request with `reading_pace=a` would match a large fraction of listings (\"daily\", \"chapter\", etc.) and return more results than the user expects. More importantly, `reading_pace` is a free-text field stored from listing creation (e.g., \"1 chapter per week\"), so the UI filter values are only matching substrings of that text by coincidence. If a user creates a listing with `reading_pace = \"daily pages\"`, it would match both the `pages` filter and the `day` filter simultaneously.\n\nThis is a data-model/logic consistency bug: the filter assumes `reading_pace` stores a tag/enum but the create form stores an arbitrary user-entered string. The count and totalPages shown to the user would be misleading for any search involving reading pace.\n\n---\n\n### 5. `debounceRef` and `abortRef` in `HomePage` are not cleaned up on unmount\n\n**Confidence: 82**\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/page.tsx`, lines 42–44, 95–98\n\n```ts\nconst debounceRef = useRef<ReturnType<typeof setTimeout>>(undefined);\nconst abortRef = useRef<AbortController>(undefined);\n```\n\n`fetchListings` aborts any in-flight request when a new one starts (line 48), but there is no `useEffect` cleanup that aborts the pending request or clears the debounce timer when the component unmounts. If a user navigates away during a debounced search or while a fetch is in-flight, the timeout callback will fire and call `fetch()` after the component unmounts. This will throw an `AbortError` (since the controller's signal is still pending), trigger the `.catch()` handler, and try to call `setLoading(false)` and `setError(...)` on an unmounted component.\n\nIn React 18 this no longer throws a warning (the \"setState on unmounted component\" warning was removed), but the orphaned fetch and timer still consume resources and could cause subtle bugs if the component is ever remounted within the debounce window.\n\n**Fix:** Add a cleanup effect:\n\n```ts\nuseEffect(() => {\n  return () => {\n    if (debounceRef.current) clearTimeout(debounceRef.current);\n    if (abortRef.current) abortRef.current.abort();\n  };\n}, []);\n```\n\n---\n\n### 6. `notifyGroupFull` is called *after* notifying the author join — double notification to members who just joined\n\n**Confidence: 80**\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/join/route.ts`, lines 108–112\n\n```ts\nnotifyListingAuthor(listingId, session.displayName || \"Someone\");\n\nif (result.count === result.maxSize) {\n  notifyGroupFull(listingId);\n}\n```\n\n`notifyListingAuthor` creates a `new_member` notification for the author. `notifyGroupFull` (called immediately after when the group just filled) then notifies *all members* including the author again. The author therefore receives two notifications in rapid succession when they are the last person to see someone join: one `new_member` and one `group_full`. This is functionally acceptable, but the `group_full` notification sent to the new joiner (the person who just joined) is also sent to the author via `notifyGroupFull`'s loop over `listing_members`, which includes the author. So the author gets both.\n\nMore concretely: the author is a member (auto-joined), so `notifyGroupFull` includes them. This means the author always gets a `group_full` notification in addition to the `new_member` notification. Two emails fire for a single join event.\n\nThis is a UX/logic issue rather than a security or data-integrity bug, but it will be noticeable every time a group fills.\n\n---\n\n## Summary\n\n| # | Severity | Issue | File |\n|---|----------|-------|------|\n| 1 | Critical | Stale `max_group_size` read inside `approveTransaction` | `/app/api/listings/[id]/applications/route.ts:67,84` |\n| 2 | Critical | No `ON DELETE CASCADE` — user deletion orphans or blocks all related data | `/lib/db.ts` (schema) |\n| 3 | Important | `notifyGroupFull` relies on author being in `listing_members` with no schema guarantee | `/lib/notifications.ts:127-128` |\n| 4 | Important | `reading_pace` LIKE filter vs free-text field causes misleading pagination counts | `/app/api/listings/route.ts:46-49` |\n| 5 | Important | Missing `useEffect` cleanup for debounce timer and AbortController on unmount | `/app/page.tsx:42-44,95-98` |\n| 6 | Important | Author receives duplicate notifications (new_member + group_full) when group fills | `/app/api/listings/[id]/join/route.ts:108-112` |"}],"totalDurationMs":108682,"totalTokens":80029,"totalToolUseCount":33,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":733,"cache_read_input_tokens":76793,"output_tokens":2502,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":733},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[],"speed":"standard"}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01Qx1tvUVWT7SN6PbD91fn5H","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→import { notifyApplicationDecision, notifyGroupFull } from \"@/lib/notifications\";\n     5→\n     6→export async function PATCH(\n     7→  req: NextRequest,\n     8→  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n     9→) {\n    10→  const session = await requireAuth();\n    11→  if (!session) {\n    12→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n    13→  }\n    14→\n    15→  const { id } = await params;\n    16→  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n    17→  if (isNaN(listingId)) {\n    18→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid listing ID\" }, { status: 400 });\n    19→  }\n    20→\n    21→  const listing = db\n    22→    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    23→    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n    24→\n    25→  if (!listing) {\n    26→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n    27→  }\n    28→\n    29→  if (listing.author_id !== session.userId) {\n    30→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Only the organizer can manage applications\" }, { status: 403 });\n    31→  }\n    32→\n    33→  const body = await req.json();\n    34→  const { applicationId, action } = body;\n    35→\n    36→  if (!applicationId || typeof applicationId !== \"number\" || !Number.isInteger(applicationId) || applicationId < 1 || ![\"approve\", \"reject\"].includes(action)) {\n    37→    return NextResponse.json(\n    38→      { error: \"Invalid request. Provide applicationId and action (approve/reject).\" },\n    39→      { status: 400 }\n    40→    );\n    41→  }\n    42→\n    43→  // Verify application belongs to this listing and is pending\n    44→  const application = db\n    45→    .prepare(\n    46→      \"SELECT * FROM listing_applications WHERE id = ? AND listing_id = ? AND status = 'pending'\"\n    47→    )\n    48→    .get(applicationId, listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n    49→\n    50→  if (!application) {\n    51→    return NextResponse.json(\n    52→      { error: \"Application not found or already decided\" },\n    53→      { status: 404 }\n    54→    );\n    55→  }\n    56→\n    57→  const applicantUserId = application.user_id as number;\n    58→\n    59→  if (action === \"approve\") {\n    60→    // Use transaction to ensure atomicity\n    61→    const approveTransaction = db.transaction(() => {\n    62→      // Check if group is already full\n    63→      const { count } = db\n    64→        .prepare(\"SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\")\n    65→        .get(listingId) as { count: number };\n    66→\n    67→      if (count >= (listing.max_group_size as number)) {\n    68→        throw new Error(\"Group is already full\");\n    69→      }\n    70→\n    71→      // Update application status\n    72→      db.prepare(\n    73→        \"UPDATE listing_applications SET status = 'approved', decided_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ?\"\n    74→      ).run(applicationId);\n    75→\n    76→      // Add to members\n    77→      db.prepare(\n    78→        \"INSERT INTO listing_members (listing_id, user_id) VALUES (?, ?)\"\n    79→      ).run(listingId, applicantUserId);\n    80→\n    81→      // Check if group is now full\n    82→      const newCount = count + 1;\n    83→      let rejectedApps: { id: number; user_id: number }[] = [];\n    84→      if (newCount >= (listing.max_group_size as number)) {\n    85→        db.prepare(\"UPDATE listings SET is_full = 1 WHERE id = ?\").run(listingId);\n    86→\n    87→        // Reject remaining pending applications inside the transaction\n    88→        rejectedApps = db\n    89→          .prepare(\n    90→            \"SELECT id, user_id FROM listing_applications WHERE listing_id = ? AND status = 'pending'\"\n    91→          )\n    92→          .all(listingId) as { id: number; user_id: number }[];\n    93→\n    94→        for (const app of rejectedApps) {\n    95→          db.prepare(\n    96→            \"UPDATE listing_applications SET status = 'rejected', decided_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ?\"\n    97→          ).run(app.id);\n    98→        }\n    99→\n   100→        return { isFull: true, rejectedApps };\n   101→      }\n   102→      return { isFull: false, rejectedApps };\n   103→    });\n   104→\n   105→    try {\n   106→      const result = approveTransaction();\n   107→\n   108→      // Send notifications outside the transaction (non-critical)\n   109→      try {\n   110→        notifyApplicationDecision(applicantUserId, listingId, \"approved\");\n   111→\n   112→        if (result.isFull) {\n   113→          notifyGroupFull(listingId);\n   114→          for (const app of result.rejectedApps) {\n   115→            notifyApplicationDecision(app.user_id, listingId, \"rejected\");\n   116→          }\n   117→        }\n   118→      } catch (notifErr) {\n   119→        console.error(\"Notification error (approval succeeded):\", notifErr);\n   120→      }\n   121→\n   122→      return NextResponse.json({ ok: true });\n   123→    } catch (err) {\n   124→      const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : \"Failed to approve\";\n   125→      return NextResponse.json({ error: message }, { status: 400 });\n   126→    }\n   127→  } else {\n   128→    // Reject\n   129→    db.prepare(\n   130→      \"UPDATE listing_applications SET status = 'rejected', decided_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ?\"\n   131→    ).run(applicationId);\n   132→\n   133→    notifyApplicationDecision(applicantUserId, listingId, \"rejected\");\n   134→\n   135→    return NextResponse.json({ ok: true });\n   136→  }\n   137→}\n   138→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"bbf58929-c3f8-4e2d-8641-5abb9b9479a7","uuid":"707eeab3-220a-4802-8764-ea964f5d33ad","timestamp":"2026-03-24T08:03:53.796Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/applications/route.ts","content":"import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\nimport db from \"@/lib/db\";\nimport { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\nimport { notifyApplicationDecision, notifyGroupFull } from \"@/lib/notifications\";\n\nexport async function PATCH(\n  req: NextRequest,\n  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n) {\n  const session = await requireAuth();\n  if (!session) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n  }\n\n  const { id } = await params;\n  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n  if (isNaN(listingId)) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid listing ID\" }, { status: 400 });\n  }\n\n  const listing = db\n    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n\n  if (!listing) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n  }\n\n  if (listing.author_id !== session.userId) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Only the organizer can manage applications\" }, { status: 403 });\n  }\n\n  const body = await req.json();\n  const { applicationId, action } = body;\n\n  if (!applicationId || typeof applicationId !== \"number\" || !Number.isInteger(applicationId) || applicationId < 1 || ![\"approve\", \"reject\"].includes(action)) {\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"Invalid request. Provide applicationId and action (approve/reject).\" },\n      { status: 400 }\n    );\n  }\n\n  // Verify application belongs to this listing and is pending\n  const application = db\n    .prepare(\n      \"SELECT * FROM listing_applications WHERE id = ? AND listing_id = ? AND status = 'pending'\"\n    )\n    .get(applicationId, listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n\n  if (!application) {\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"Application not found or already decided\" },\n      { status: 404 }\n    );\n  }\n\n  const applicantUserId = application.user_id as number;\n\n  if (action === \"approve\") {\n    // Use transaction to ensure atomicity\n    const approveTransaction = db.transaction(() => {\n      // Check if group is already full\n      const { count } = db\n        .prepare(\"SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\")\n        .get(listingId) as { count: number };\n\n      if (count >= (listing.max_group_size as number)) {\n        throw new Error(\"Group is already full\");\n      }\n\n      // Update application status\n      db.prepare(\n        \"UPDATE listing_applications SET status = 'approved', decided_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ?\"\n      ).run(applicationId);\n\n      // Add to members\n      db.prepare(\n        \"INSERT INTO listing_members (listing_id, user_id) VALUES (?, ?)\"\n      ).run(listingId, applicantUserId);\n\n      // Check if group is now full\n      const newCount = count + 1;\n      let rejectedApps: { id: number; user_id: number }[] = [];\n      if (newCount >= (listing.max_group_size as number)) {\n        db.prepare(\"UPDATE listings SET is_full = 1 WHERE id = ?\").run(listingId);\n\n        // Reject remaining pending applications inside the transaction\n        rejectedApps = db\n          .prepare(\n            \"SELECT id, user_id FROM listing_applications WHERE listing_id = ? AND status = 'pending'\"\n          )\n          .all(listingId) as { id: number; user_id: number }[];\n\n        for (const app of rejectedApps) {\n          db.prepare(\n            \"UPDATE listing_applications SET status = 'rejected', decided_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ?\"\n          ).run(app.id);\n        }\n\n        return { isFull: true, rejectedApps };\n      }\n      return { isFull: false, rejectedApps };\n    });\n\n    try {\n      const result = approveTransaction();\n\n      // Send notifications outside the transaction (non-critical)\n      try {\n        notifyApplicationDecision(applicantUserId, listingId, \"approved\");\n\n        if (result.isFull) {\n          notifyGroupFull(listingId);\n          for (const app of result.rejectedApps) {\n            notifyApplicationDecision(app.user_id, listingId, \"rejected\");\n          }\n        }\n      } catch (notifErr) {\n        console.error(\"Notification error (approval succeeded):\", notifErr);\n      }\n\n      return NextResponse.json({ ok: true });\n    } catch (err) {\n      const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : \"Failed to approve\";\n      return NextResponse.json({ error: message }, { status: 400 });\n    }\n  } else {\n    // Reject\n    db.prepare(\n      \"UPDATE listing_applications SET status = 'rejected', decided_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ?\"\n    ).run(applicationId);\n\n    notifyApplicationDecision(applicantUserId, listingId, \"rejected\");\n\n    return NextResponse.json({ ok: true });\n  }\n}\n","numLines":138,"startLine":1,"totalLines":138}}}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01LPPpZNA3CWE1MvhGXThCPN","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→\"use client\";\n     2→\n     3→import { useEffect, useState, useCallback, useRef } from \"react\";\n     4→import Link from \"next/link\";\n     5→import BookCover from \"@/components/BookCover\";\n     6→\n     7→interface Listing {\n     8→  id: number;\n     9→  book_title: string;\n    10→  book_author: string;\n    11→  book_cover_url: string;\n    12→  language: string;\n    13→  reading_pace: string;\n    14→  start_date: string;\n    15→  meeting_format: string;\n    16→  max_group_size: number;\n    17→  requires_approval: number;\n    18→  platform_preference: string;\n    19→  member_count: number;\n    20→  author_name: string;\n    21→  created_at: string;\n    22→}\n    23→\n    24→export default function HomePage() {\n    25→  const [listings, setListings] = useState<Listing[]>([]);\n    26→  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true);\n    27→  const [error, setError] = useState<string | null>(null);\n    28→\n    29→  // Search & filter state\n    30→  const [searchQuery, setSearchQuery] = useState(\"\");\n    31→  const [meetingFormat, setMeetingFormat] = useState(\"\");\n    32→  const [readingPace, setReadingPace] = useState(\"\");\n    33→  const [startDateFrom, setStartDateFrom] = useState(\"\");\n    34→  const [sort, setSort] = useState(\"newest\");\n    35→  const [filtersOpen, setFiltersOpen] = useState(false);\n    36→\n    37→  // Pagination state\n    38→  const [page, setPage] = useState(1);\n    39→  const [totalPages, setTotalPages] = useState(1);\n    40→  const [totalResults, setTotalResults] = useState(0);\n    41→\n    42→  const debounceRef = useRef<ReturnType<typeof setTimeout>>(undefined);\n    43→  const abortRef = useRef<AbortController>(undefined);\n    44→\n    45→  const fetchListings = useCallback(\n    46→    (query: string, format: string, sortBy: string, pace?: string, dateFrom?: string, pageNum?: number) => {\n    47→      // Abort any in-flight request to prevent stale responses overwriting newer ones\n    48→      if (abortRef.current) abortRef.current.abort();\n    49→      const controller = new AbortController();\n    50→      abortRef.current = controller;\n    51→\n    52→      setLoading(true);\n    53→      const params = new URLSearchParams();\n    54→      if (query) params.set(\"q\", query);\n    55→      if (format) params.set(\"meeting_format\", format);\n    56→      if (pace) params.set(\"reading_pace\", pace);\n    57→      if (dateFrom) params.set(\"start_date_from\", dateFrom);\n    58→      if (sortBy) params.set(\"sort\", sortBy);\n    59→      if (pageNum && pageNum > 1) params.set(\"page\", String(pageNum));\n    60→\n    61→      const qs = params.toString();\n    62→      fetch(`/api/listings${qs ? `?${qs}` : \"\"}`, { signal: controller.signal })\n    63→        .then((r) => {\n    64→          if (!r.ok) throw new Error(\"Failed to load listings\");\n    65→          return r.json();\n    66→        })\n    67→        .then((data) => {\n    68→          if (controller.signal.aborted) return;\n    69→          setListings(data.listings || []);\n    70→          if (data.pagination) {\n    71→            setTotalPages(data.pagination.totalPages || 1);\n    72→            setTotalResults(data.pagination.total || 0);\n    73→          }\n    74→          setError(null);\n    75→          setLoading(false);\n    76→        })\n    77→        .catch((err) => {\n    78→          if ((err as Error).name === \"AbortError\") return;\n    79→          setError(err.message || \"Could not load listings. Please try again.\");\n    80→          setLoading(false);\n    81→        });\n    82→    },\n    83→    []\n    84→  );\n    85→\n    86→  // Initial fetch\n    87→  useEffect(() => {\n    88→    fetchListings(\"\", \"\", \"newest\", \"\", \"\", 1);\n    89→  }, [fetchListings]);\n    90→\n    91→  // Debounced search\n    92→  const handleSearchChange = (value: string) => {\n    93→    setSearchQuery(value);\n    94→    setPage(1);\n    95→    if (debounceRef.current) clearTimeout(debounceRef.current);\n    96→    debounceRef.current = setTimeout(() => {\n    97→      fetchListings(value, meetingFormat, sort, readingPace, startDateFrom, 1);\n    98→    }, 400);\n    99→  };\n   100→\n   101→  // Immediate filter changes\n   102→  const handleFormatChange = (value: string) => {\n   103→    setMeetingFormat(value);\n   104→    setPage(1);\n   105→    fetchListings(searchQuery, value, sort, readingPace, startDateFrom, 1);\n   106→  };\n   107→\n   108→  const handlePaceChange = (value: string) => {\n   109→    setReadingPace(value);\n   110→    setPage(1);\n   111→    fetchListings(searchQuery, meetingFormat, sort, value, startDateFrom, 1);\n   112→  };\n   113→\n   114→  const handleStartDateFromChange = (value: string) => {\n   115→    setStartDateFrom(value);\n   116→    setPage(1);\n   117→    fetchListings(searchQuery, meetingFormat, sort, readingPace, value, 1);\n   118→  };\n   119→\n   120→  const handleSortChange = (value: string) => {\n   121→    setSort(value);\n   122→    setPage(1);\n   123→    fetchListings(searchQuery, meetingFormat, value, readingPace, startDateFrom, 1);\n   124→  };\n   125→\n   126→  const handlePageChange = (newPage: number) => {\n   127→    setPage(newPage);\n   128→    fetchListings(searchQuery, meetingFormat, sort, readingPace, startDateFrom, newPage);\n   129→    window.scrollTo({ top: 0, behavior: \"smooth\" });\n   130→  };\n   131→\n   132→  const clearFilters = () => {\n   133→    setSearchQuery(\"\");\n   134→    setMeetingFormat(\"\");\n   135→    setReadingPace(\"\");\n   136→    setStartDateFrom(\"\");\n   137→    setSort(\"newest\");\n   138→    setPage(1);\n   139→    fetchListings(\"\", \"\", \"newest\", \"\", \"\", 1);\n   140→  };\n   141→\n   142→  const hasActiveFilters = searchQuery || meetingFormat || readingPace || startDateFrom || sort !== \"newest\";\n   143→\n   144→  const formatMeetingType = (f: string) => {\n   145→    const labels: Record<string, string> = {\n   146→      voice: \"Voice calls\",\n   147→      text: \"Text chat\",\n   148→      mixed: \"Mixed\",\n   149→    };\n   150→    return labels[f] || f;\n   151→  };\n   152→\n   153→  return (\n   154→    <div>\n   155→      <div className=\"text-center mb-6 sm:mb-8 mt-2 sm:mt-4\">\n   156→        <h1 className=\"text-2xl sm:text-4xl mb-2 sm:mb-3\" style={{ color: \"var(--color-text)\" }}>\n   157→          Find your reading companion\n   158→        </h1>\n   159→        <p\n   160→          className=\"text-sm sm:text-lg max-w-xl mx-auto\"\n   161→          style={{\n   162→            color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\",\n   163→            fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\",\n   164→          }}\n   165→        >\n   166→          Browse open reading groups, join one that matches your pace, and\n   167→          connect on Telegram to read together.\n   168→        </p>\n   169→      </div>\n   170→\n   171→      {/* Search & Filter Bar */}\n   172→      <div className=\"mb-6\">\n   173→        <div className=\"flex gap-3 items-center\">\n   174→          <div className=\"flex-1 relative\">\n   175→            <input\n   176→              type=\"text\"\n   177→              placeholder=\"Search by book title or author...\"\n   178→              value={searchQuery}\n   179→              onChange={(e) => handleSearchChange(e.target.value)}\n   180→              className=\"input-field\"\n   181→              style={{ paddingLeft: \"2.5rem\" }}\n   182→            />\n   183→            <svg\n   184→              aria-hidden=\"true\"\n   185→              style={{\n   186→                position: \"absolute\",\n   187→                left: \"0.75rem\",\n   188→                top: \"50%\",\n   189→                transform: \"translateY(-50%)\",\n   190→                color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\",\n   191→                width: \"1.125rem\",\n   192→                height: \"1.125rem\",\n   193→                pointerEvents: \"none\",\n   194→              }}\n   195→              fill=\"none\"\n   196→              stroke=\"currentColor\"\n   197→              strokeWidth={2}\n   198→              viewBox=\"0 0 24 24\"\n   199→            >\n   200→              <circle cx={11} cy={11} r={8} />\n   201→              <line x1={21} y1={21} x2={16.65} y2={16.65} />\n   202→            </svg>\n   203→          </div>\n   204→          <button\n   205→            onClick={() => setFiltersOpen(!filtersOpen)}\n   206→            className=\"btn-secondary\"\n   207→            aria-expanded={filtersOpen}\n   208→            aria-controls=\"filter-panel\"\n   209→            style={{\n   210→              display: \"flex\",\n   211→              alignItems: \"center\",\n   212→              gap: \"0.375rem\",\n   213→              whiteSpace: \"nowrap\",\n   214→              position: \"relative\",\n   215→            }}\n   216→          >\n   217→            <svg\n   218→              aria-hidden=\"true\"\n   219→              width=\"16\"\n   220→              height=\"16\"\n   221→              viewBox=\"0 0 24 24\"\n   222→              fill=\"none\"\n   223→              stroke=\"currentColor\"\n   224→              strokeWidth={2}\n   225→            >\n   226→              <line x1={4} y1={6} x2={20} y2={6} />\n   227→              <line x1={7} y1={12} x2={17} y2={12} />\n   228→              <line x1={10} y1={18} x2={14} y2={18} />\n   229→            </svg>\n   230→            Filters\n   231→            {hasActiveFilters && (\n   232→              <span\n   233→                aria-hidden=\"true\"\n   234→                style={{\n   235→                  width: \"0.5rem\",\n   236→                  height: \"0.5rem\",\n   237→                  borderRadius: \"50%\",\n   238→                  backgroundColor: \"var(--color-accent)\",\n   239→                  position: \"absolute\",\n   240→                  top: \"0.375rem\",\n   241→                  right: \"0.375rem\",\n   242→                }}\n   243→              />\n   244→            )}\n   245→          </button>\n   246→        </div>\n   247→\n   248→        {/* Filter panel */}\n   249→        {filtersOpen && (\n   250→          <div\n   251→            id=\"filter-panel\"\n   252→            className=\"card mt-3\"\n   253→            style={{ padding: \"1rem 1.25rem\" }}\n   254→          >\n   255→            <div\n   256→              className=\"grid grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-4 gap-3 sm:gap-4 items-end\"\n   257→              style={{ fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}\n   258→            >\n   259→              <div>\n   260→                <label\n   261→                  className=\"text-xs font-semibold mb-1 block\"\n   262→                  style={{\n   263→                    color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\",\n   264→                    textTransform: \"uppercase\",\n   265→                    letterSpacing: \"0.05em\",\n   266→                  }}\n   267→                >\n   268→                  Meeting format\n   269→                </label>\n   270→                <select\n   271→                  value={meetingFormat}\n   272→                  onChange={(e) => handleFormatChange(e.target.value)}\n   273→                  className=\"input-field\"\n   274→                  style={{ fontSize: \"0.875rem\", padding: \"0.5rem 0.75rem\" }}\n   275→                >\n   276→                  <option value=\"\">All formats</option>\n   277→                  <option value=\"voice\">Voice calls</option>\n   278→                  <option value=\"text\">Text chat</option>\n   279→                  <option value=\"mixed\">Mixed</option>\n   280→                </select>\n   281→              </div>\n   282→\n   283→              <div>\n   284→                <label\n   285→                  className=\"text-xs font-semibold mb-1 block\"\n   286→                  style={{\n   287→                    color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\",\n   288→                    textTransform: \"uppercase\",\n   289→                    letterSpacing: \"0.05em\",\n   290→                  }}\n   291→                >\n   292→                  Reading pace\n   293→                </label>\n   294→                <select\n   295→                  value={readingPace}\n   296→                  onChange={(e) => handlePaceChange(e.target.value)}\n   297→                  className=\"input-field\"\n   298→                  style={{ fontSize: \"0.875rem\", padding: \"0.5rem 0.75rem\" }}\n   299→                >\n   300→                  <option value=\"\">All paces</option>\n   301→                  <option value=\"chapter\">Chapter-based</option>\n   302→                  <option value=\"pages\">Pages-based</option>\n   303→                  <option value=\"week\">Weekly</option>\n   304→                  <option value=\"day\">Daily</option>\n   305→                </select>\n   306→              </div>\n   307→\n   308→              <div>\n   309→                <label\n   310→                  className=\"text-xs font-semibold mb-1 block\"\n   311→                  style={{\n   312→                    color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\",\n   313→                    textTransform: \"uppercase\",\n   314→                    letterSpacing: \"0.05em\",\n   315→                  }}\n   316→                >\n   317→                  Starting from\n   318→                </label>\n   319→                <input\n   320→                  type=\"date\"\n   321→                  value={startDateFrom}\n padding: \"0.5rem 0.75rem\" }}\n   325→                />\n   326→              </div>\n   327→\n   328→              <div>\n   329→                <label\n   330→                  className=\"text-xs font-semibold mb-1 block\"\n   331→                  style={{\n   332→                    color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\",\n   333→                    textTransform: \"uppercase\",\n   334→
