{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_017b6Uqr9Mi2jw12AJmL4zUV","type":"tool_result","content":"    85→) {\n    86→  const listing = db\n    87→    .prepare(\n    88→      \"SELECT l.author_id, l.book_title FROM listings l WHERE l.id = ?\"\n    89→    )\n    90→    .get(listingId) as { author_id: number; book_title: string } | undefined;\n    91→\n    92→  if (listing) {\n    93→    createNotification(\n    94→      listing.author_id,\n    95→      listingId,\n    96→      \"application_received\",\n    97→      `${applicantName} applied to join your reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\"`\n    98→    );\n    99→  }\n   100→}\n   101→\n   102→export function notifyApplicationDecision(\n   103→  userId: number,\n   104→  listingId: number,\n   105→  decision: \"approved\" | \"rejected\"\n   106→) {\n   107→  const listing = db\n   108→    .prepare(\"SELECT book_title FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n   109→    .get(listingId) as { book_title: string } | undefined;\n   110→\n   111→  if (listing) {\n   112→    const type = decision === \"approved\" ? \"application_approved\" : \"application_rejected\";\n   113→    const message =\n   114→      decision === \"approved\"\n   115→        ? `Your application to join \"${listing.book_title}\" was approved!`\n   116→        : `Your application to join \"${listing.book_title}\" was not accepted.`;\n   117→    createNotification(userId, listingId, type, message);\n   118→  }\n   119→}\n   120→\n   121→export function notifyGroupFull(listingId: number) {\n   122→  const listing = db\n   123→    .prepare(\"SELECT book_title, platform_preference, author_id FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n   124→    .get(listingId) as { book_title: string; platform_preference: string; author_id: number } | undefined;\n   125→  // Include all members (including the author) — author needs to know group is full to create the chat\n   126→  const members = db\n   127→    .prepare(\"SELECT user_id FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\")\n   128→    .all(listingId) as { user_id: number }[];\n   129→\n   130→  if (listing) {\n   131→    const platform = listing.platform_preference === \"discord\" ? \"Discord\" : \"Telegram\";\n   132→    const botConfigured = listing.platform_preference === \"discord\"\n   133→      ? !!process.env.DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN\n   134→      : !!process.env.TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN;\n   135→    const message = botConfigured\n   136→      ? `The reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\" is now full! The ${platform} group will be set up automatically.`\n   137→      : `The reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\" is now full! The organizer will share a ${platform} link soon.`;\n   138→\n   139→    for (const member of members) {\n   140→      createNotification(\n   141→        member.user_id,\n   142→        listingId,\n   143→        \"group_full\",\n   144→        message\n   145→      );\n   146→    }\n   147→  }\n   148→}\n   149→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"bbf58929-c3f8-4e2d-8641-5abb9b9479a7","uuid":"4b7cae5b-c301-4126-9627-b8aacd2e823d","timestamp":"2026-03-24T08:04:19.701Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/notifications.ts","content":") {\n  const listing = db\n    .prepare(\n      \"SELECT l.author_id, l.book_title FROM listings l WHERE l.id = ?\"\n    )\n    .get(listingId) as { author_id: number; book_title: string } | undefined;\n\n  if (listing) {\n    createNotification(\n      listing.author_id,\n      listingId,\n      \"application_received\",\n      `${applicantName} applied to join your reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\"`\n    );\n  }\n}\n\nexport function notifyApplicationDecision(\n  userId: number,\n  listingId: number,\n  decision: \"approved\" | \"rejected\"\n) {\n  const listing = db\n    .prepare(\"SELECT book_title FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    .get(listingId) as { book_title: string } | undefined;\n\n  if (listing) {\n    const type = decision === \"approved\" ? \"application_approved\" : \"application_rejected\";\n    const message =\n      decision === \"approved\"\n        ? `Your application to join \"${listing.book_title}\" was approved!`\n        : `Your application to join \"${listing.book_title}\" was not accepted.`;\n    createNotification(userId, listingId, type, message);\n  }\n}\n\nexport function notifyGroupFull(listingId: number) {\n  const listing = db\n    .prepare(\"SELECT book_title, platform_preference, author_id FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    .get(listingId) as { book_title: string; platform_preference: string; author_id: number } | undefined;\n  // Include all members (including the author) — author needs to know group is full to create the chat\n  const members = db\n    .prepare(\"SELECT user_id FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\")\n    .all(listingId) as { user_id: number }[];\n\n  if (listing) {\n    const platform = listing.platform_preference === \"discord\" ? \"Discord\" : \"Telegram\";\n    const botConfigured = listing.platform_preference === \"discord\"\n      ? !!process.env.DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN\n      : !!process.env.TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN;\n    const message = botConfigured\n      ? `The reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\" is now full! The ${platform} group will be set up automatically.`\n      : `The reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\" is now full! The organizer will share a ${platform} link soon.`;\n\n    for (const member of members) {\n      createNotification(\n        member.user_id,\n        listingId,\n        \"group_full\",\n        message\n      );\n    }\n  }\n}\n","numLines":65,"startLine":85,"totalLines":149}}}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_014ZaSZpZEiuGfeqCqTyJKYW","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"thinking","thinking":"Looking at the join route more carefully:\n\n```\nnotifyListingAuthor(listingId, session.displayName || \"Someone\");  // notifies author with \"new_member\"\n\nif (result.count === result.maxSize) {\n  notifyGroupFull(listingId);  // notifies ALL members including author with \"group_full\"\n}\n```\n\nThe issue is the author gets both a \"new_member\" notification AND a \"group_full\" notification when the group fills. This is actually correct behavior - they ARE two different events. The author should know someone joined AND that the group is full.\n\nActually wait - these are two separate notification types with different messages. \n\nThe `new_member` notification says \"X joined your reading group\" while `group_full` says \"the group is full\" - they convey different information, so both seem reasonable. But the reviewer's concern about receiving two notifications for a single join event is valid from a UX perspective. The `group_full` notification is probably more critical since it prompts the author to set up the communication channel, making the `new_member` notification somewhat redundant when the group fills. I think the cleaner approach is to suppress the `new_member` notification for the author when the group reaches capacity and just send the `group_full` notification instead, which is more actionable.","signature":""}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1028,"cache_read_input_tokens":50075,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":1028},"output_tokens":8,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"bbf58929-c3f8-4e2d-8641-5abb9b9479a7","uuid":"3aaa7559-95c9-4a53-ae73-9ba5289a145b"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_011P8Vne9C7kmc1GSTWuR6aV","type":"tool_result","content":"The file /root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/join/route.ts has been updated successfully."}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"bbf58929-c3f8-4e2d-8641-5abb9b9479a7","uuid":"cf3305cc-f0b1-4d42-af76-c86e1f1efcb9","timestamp":"2026-03-24T08:04:34.411Z","tool_use_result":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/join/route.ts","oldString":"  // Notify outside the transaction (fire-and-forget)\n  notifyListingAuthor(listingId, session.displayName || \"Someone\");\n\n  if (result.count === result.maxSize) {\n    notifyGroupFull(listingId);\n  }","newString":"  // Notify outside the transaction (fire-and-forget)\n  // When group just filled, skip new_member notification for the author\n  // since they'll receive the more important group_full notification instead\n  if (result.count === result.maxSize) {\n    notifyGroupFull(listingId);\n  } else {\n    notifyListingAuthor(listingId, session.displayName || \"Someone\");\n  }","originalFile":"import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\nimport db from \"@/lib/db\";\nimport { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\nimport { notifyListingAuthor, notifyGroupFull } from \"@/lib/notifications\";\n\nexport async function POST(\n  _req: NextRequest,\n  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n) {\n  const session = await requireAuth();\n  if (!session) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n  }\n\n  const { id } = await params;\n  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n  if (isNaN(listingId)) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid listing ID\" }, { status: 400 });\n  }\n\n  const listing = db\n    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n\n  if (!listing) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n  }\n\n  // Author is already a member — don't allow re-joining\n  if (listing.author_id === session.userId) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"You are the organizer of this group\" }, { status: 400 });\n  }\n\n  if (listing.requires_approval) {\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"This group requires approval. Please apply instead.\" },\n      { status: 400 }\n    );\n  }\n\n  if (listing.is_full) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"This group is already full\" }, { status: 400 });\n  }\n\n  // Check if already a member\n  const existing = db\n    .prepare(\n      \"SELECT 1 FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ? AND user_id = ?\"\n    )\n    .get(listingId, session.userId);\n\n  if (existing) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"You are already a member\" }, { status: 400 });\n  }\n\n  // Check if user has a pending application (e.g., requires_approval was toggled off)\n  const pendingApp = db\n    .prepare(\n      \"SELECT 1 FROM listing_applications WHERE listing_id = ? AND user_id = ? AND status = 'pending'\"\n    )\n    .get(listingId, session.userId);\n\n  if (pendingApp) {\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"You already have a pending application for this group\" },\n      { status: 400 }\n    );\n  }\n\n  // Wrap count check + insert in a transaction to prevent race conditions\n  // Re-read max_group_size inside the transaction to avoid stale data from concurrent PATCH\n  const joinTransaction = db.transaction(() => {\n    const freshListing = db\n      .prepare(\"SELECT max_group_size FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n      .get(listingId) as { max_group_size: number } | undefined;\n\n    if (!freshListing) {\n      return { error: \"Listing not found\", count: 0, maxSize: 0 };\n    }\n\n    const { count } = db\n      .prepare(\"SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\")\n      .get(listingId) as { count: number };\n\n    if (count >= freshListing.max_group_size) {\n      return { error: \"This group is already full\", count, maxSize: freshListing.max_group_size };\n    }\n\n    db.prepare(\n      \"INSERT INTO listing_members (listing_id, user_id) VALUES (?, ?)\"\n    ).run(listingId, session.userId);\n\n    const newCount = count + 1;\n    if (newCount >= freshListing.max_group_size) {\n      db.prepare(\"UPDATE listings SET is_full = 1 WHERE id = ?\").run(listingId);\n    }\n\n    return { error: null, count: newCount, maxSize: freshListing.max_group_size };\n  });\n\n  const result = joinTransaction();\n\n  if (result.error) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: result.error }, { status: 400 });\n  }\n\n  // Notify outside the transaction (fire-and-forget)\n  notifyListingAuthor(listingId, session.displayName || \"Someone\");\n\n  if (result.count === result.maxSize) {\n    notifyGroupFull(listingId);\n  }\n\n  return NextResponse.json({ ok: true, memberCount: result.count });\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":105,"oldLines":10,"newStart":105,"newLines":12,"lines":["   }"," ","   // Notify outside the transaction (fire-and-forget)","-  notifyListingAuthor(listingId, session.displayName || \"Someone\");","-","+  // When group just filled, skip new_member notification for the author","+  // since they'll receive the more important group_full notification instead","   if (result.count === result.maxSize) {","     notifyGroupFull(listingId);","+  } else {","+    notifyListingAuthor(listingId, session.displayName || \"Someone\");","   }"," ","   return NextResponse.json({ ok: true, memberCount: result.count });"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_01R9owE3QTPsGdMNRgRrZDR8","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_014V6vGq8ynRnW3xTfzGTeVb","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"export GH_TOKEN=$(grep GH_TOKEN /root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/.env | cut -d= -f2) && gh issue create --title \"Code review round 11: stale transaction data, unmount cleanup, duplicate notifications\" --label \"bug,P1,approved\" --body \"## Bugs found in code review round 11\n\n1. **Stale \\`max_group_size\\` in applications route** (CRITICAL) — The \\`listing\\` object is fetched outside the transaction, but \\`max_group_size\\` is used inside it. A concurrent PATCH could change the value, allowing one extra member. Fix: re-read \\`max_group_size\\` inside the transaction (matching the join route pattern).\n\n2. **Missing \\`useEffect\\` cleanup on HomePage** (IMPORTANT) — \\`debounceRef\\` and \\`abortRef\\` are not cleaned up on unmount. Navigating away during a debounced search leaves orphaned timers and in-flight fetches. Fix: add cleanup effect.\n\n3. **Duplicate author notifications on group fill** (IMPORTANT) — When the last person joins via the join route, the author receives both a \\`new_member\\` notification and a \\`group_full\\` notification. Fix: skip \\`new_member\\` when group fills, since \\`group_full\\` is more informative.\" 2>&1","description":"Create issue for code review round 11"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":135,"cache_read_input_tokens":53688,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":135},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"bbf58929-c3f8-4e2d-8641-5abb9b9479a7","uuid":"0eace072-7b68-4cd2-bc43-001367501170"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_01PmK6QgLTL4tDooQViN5kaT","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01FCMG1fC3wU47eB3zgRNUXd","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"export GH_TOKEN=$(grep GH_TOKEN /root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/.env | cut -d= -f2) && gh pr create --title \"fix: code review round 11 — stale data, unmount cleanup, duplicate notifications\" --body \"$(cat <<'EOF'\n## Summary\n- **Stale `max_group_size` in approve transaction** (CRITICAL) — The applications route read `max_group_size` outside the transaction and used the stale value inside. A concurrent PATCH could change it, allowing extra members. Now re-reads inside the transaction, matching the join route pattern.\n- **Missing `useEffect` cleanup on HomePage** (IMPORTANT) — Debounce timer and AbortController were not cleaned up on unmount, leaving orphaned fetches after navigation. Added cleanup effect.\n- **Duplicate author notifications on group fill** (IMPORTANT) — Author received both `new_member` and `group_full` when last person joined. Now only sends `group_full` which is more informative.\n\nCloses #96\n\n## Test plan\n- [x] All 198 tests pass\n- [x] ESLint clean\n- [x] Build passes\n- [ ] Verify approve flow still works correctly with transaction fix\n- [ ] Verify HomePage navigation doesn't leave orphaned fetches\n- [ ] Verify author gets single notification when group fills\n\n🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)\nEOF\n)\" 2>&1","description":"Create pull request"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":375,"cache_read_input_tokens":57955,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":375},"output_tokens":64,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"bbf58929-c3f8-4e2d-8641-5abb9b9479a7","uuid":"24708437-6c40-4cc6-9324-3f235399341b"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_016mBzYfdJgpsf9PgQ8xidwJ","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_0115x9En6GP34F15qVxR9Qya","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/logs/session-2026-03-24-0800.md","content":"# Session Log — 2026-03-24 08:00 UTC\n\n## Summary\nCode review round 11: focused on data integrity, resource cleanup, and notification logic. Found and fixed 3 bugs. Created issue #96, implemented fixes in PR #97, CI passed, merged to main, deployed to production.\n\n## Review Findings\n- **Production**: Healthy — HTTP 200, PM2 online, 198 tests pass\n- **CI**: Green on main\n- **Open PRs**: None\n- **Open issues**: #54 (needs-human, PAT expiry ~3 days), #21 (needs-human, Telegram token)\n- **Emails**: GitHub notifications for #74 (already resolved), NomadLingo (different project), Google Workspace promo\n- **PRD sync**: All PRD features have corresponding closed issues\n- **PAT status**: Still valid, added reminder comment on #54\n\n## Work Completed\n\n### Issue #96: Code review round 11 (PR #97 — merged + deployed)\n\n3 bugs fixed:\n\n1. **Stale `max_group_size` in approve transaction** (CRITICAL) — Applications route read `max_group_size` outside the transaction closure but used it inside. A concurrent PATCH to the listing could change the value between the outer read and the transaction execution, allowing one extra member past the cap. Fixed by re-reading `max_group_size` inside the transaction, matching the pattern already used in the join route.\n\n2. **Missing `useEffect` cleanup on HomePage** (IMPORTANT) — `debounceRef` and `abortRef` were not cleaned up on unmount. Navigating away during a debounced search left orphaned timers and in-flight fetches that could call setState after unmount. Added cleanup effect to clear timeout and abort controller.\n\n3. **Duplicate author notifications on group fill** (IMPORTANT) — When the last person joined, the author received both a `new_member` notification and a `group_full` notification. Now skips the `new_member` notification when the group fills, since `group_full` is more informative and already implies the join event.\n\nAll 198 tests pass, ESLint clean, build clean, CI green.\n\n## Also Noted (not fixed this round)\n- No `ON DELETE CASCADE` on FK constraints — would need schema migration; no user deletion endpoint exists yet\n- `reading_pace` LIKE filter vs free-text field inconsistency — cosmetic, not causing data issues\n- `notifyGroupFull` depends on author being in `listing_members` — fragile invariant but holds today\n\n## Issues Monitored\n- **#54 (PAT expiry)**: ~3 days remaining — critical, needs-human. Added reminder comment.\n- **#21 (Telegram token)**: Still needs-human\n\n## Next Session Priority\n1. Check production health\n2. Monitor PAT expiry (#54) — critical\n3. Consider E2E testing, performance improvements, or new feature proposals\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":369,"cache_read_input_tokens":62444,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":369},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"bbf58929-c3f8-4e2d-8641-5abb9b9479a7","uuid":"82fa58b3-704b-4117-88e7-cba3675c7d3c"}

Created issue #96, implemented fixes in PR #97, CI passed, merged to main, deployed to production.\n\n## Review Findings\n- **Production**: Healthy — HTTP 200, PM2 online, 198 tests pass\n- **CI**: Green on main\n- **Open PRs**: None\n- **Open issues**: #54 (needs-human, PAT expiry ~3 days), #21 (needs-human, Telegram token)\n- **Emails**: GitHub notifications for #74 (already resolved), NomadLingo (different project), Google Workspace promo\n- **PRD sync**: All PRD features have corresponding closed issues\n- **PAT status**: Still valid, added reminder comment on #54\n\n## Work Completed\n\n### Issue #96: Code review round 11 (PR #97 — merged + deployed)\n\n3 bugs fixed:\n\n1. **Stale `max_group_size` in approve transaction** (CRITICAL) — Applications route read `max_group_size` outside the transaction closure but used it inside. A concurrent PATCH to the listing could change the value between the outer read and the transaction execution, allowing one extra member past the cap. Fixed by re-reading `max_group_size` inside the transaction, matching the pattern already used in the join route.\n\n2. **Missing `useEffect` cleanup on HomePage** (IMPORTANT) — `debounceRef` and `abortRef` were not cleaned up on unmount. Navigating away during a debounced search left orphaned timers and in-flight fetches that could call setState after unmount. Added cleanup effect to clear timeout and abort controller.\n\n3. **Duplicate author notifications on group fill** (IMPORTANT) — When the last person joined, the author received both a `new_member` notification and a `group_full` notification. Now skips the `new_member` notification when the group fills, since `group_full` is more informative and already implies the join event.\n\nAll 198 tests pass, ESLint clean, build clean, CI green.\n\n## Also Noted (not fixed this round)\n- No `ON DELETE CASCADE` on FK constraints — would need schema migration; no user deletion endpoint exists yet\n- `reading_pace` LIKE filter vs free-text field inconsistency — cosmetic, not causing data issues\n- `notifyGroupFull` depends on author being in `listing_members` — fragile invariant but holds today\n\n## Issues Monitored\n- **#54 (PAT expiry)**: ~3 days remaining — critical, needs-human. Added reminder comment.\n- **#21 (Telegram token)**: Still needs-human\n\n## Next Session Priority\n1. Check production health\n2. Monitor PAT expiry (#54) — critical\n3. — timing-safe secrets, type validation, stale transaction data, email HTML escaping — merged + deployed\n- #87: Code review round 6 — logout fix, is_full reset, email subjects, webhook validation, fetch abort — merged + deployed","originalFile":"# Agent Memory\n\n## Project\n- **Name**: Bookmate\n- **Repo**: https://github.com/TommyBrennan/bookmate\n- **Project Board**: Bookmate (project #1 on TommyBrennan)\n- **Tech**: Next.js 15, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS v4, SQLite, iron-session\n- **Owner**: XanderZhu (human), TommyBrennan (GH account)\n\n## Current State\n- All PRD features implemented and merged (P0, P1, P2)\n- CI pipeline live: GitHub Actions runs lint + type check + build on push/PR to main\n- ESLint flat config with typescript-eslint, react-hooks, @next/eslint-plugin-next\n- Build passes clean, lint clean (no warnings)\n- Security hardening merged (PR #32, #36)\n- **Production deployed** via PM2 on port 3000 — http://89.167.127.85:3000\n- Health endpoint: `/api/health` (DB check, uptime, stats)\n- Custom 404 page (branded, with CTA)\n- OG + Twitter Card meta tags on listing pages (book cover, title, description)\n- Loading skeletons on all routes (root, listing detail, profile, notifications)\n- Error boundaries on all routes with retry functionality\n- Focus-visible styles for keyboard accessibility\n- **Accessibility improvements** (PR #49, merged + deployed):\n  - ARIA labels, roles, expanded states on all interactive elements\n  - Keyboard navigation on BookSearch dropdown (combobox pattern)\n  - autocomplete attributes on auth forms\n  - role=\"alert\" on error messages\n  - Skip-to-content link\n  - role=\"tablist\"/tab/tabpanel on profile tabs\n  - role=\"radiogroup\"/radio on format/platform buttons\n  - role=\"checkbox\" with keyboard support on approval toggle\n\n## Production\n- **URL**: http://89.167.127.85:3000\n- **Process manager**: PM2 (via npx)\n- **Config**: `app/ecosystem.config.js`\n- **Deploy script**: `scripts/deploy.sh`\n- **Logs**: `.claude-beat/logs/pm2-{out,error}.log`\n- **No reverse proxy** (nginx requires root — not available)\n- **No HTTPS** (no domain configured, no certbot)\n- PM2 process saved: `npx pm2 save` (restores on `pm2 resurrect`)\n\n## Open PRs\n- None\n\n## Closed Recently\n- #95: Code review round 10 — FK violations, stale notifications, input validation, error UX — merged + deployed\n- #93: Code review round 9 — client-side race conditions, date bugs, error handling, ARIA — merged + deployed\n- #91: Code review round 8 — timing-safe secrets, type validation, stale transaction data, email HTML escaping — merged + deployed\n- #87: Code review round 6 — logout fix, is_full reset, email subjects, webhook validation, fetch abort — merged + deployed\n- #85: Test coverage for Telegram and Discord API routes (69 new tests) — merged\n\n## Open Issues\n- #21: Telegram bot token (needs-human) — multiple follow-up reminders sent, still waiting\n- #54: **PAT expires ~March 27** (needs-human) — GH_TOKEN renewal required, ~3 days left\n\n## Important Notes\n- GH_TOKEN loaded from `.claude-beat/.env`\n- Remote URL includes PAT: reset with `git remote set-url` if needed\n- Next.js 15 used (not 16) due to Turbopack /root permission issue\n- agent-browser WORKS if: export XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp/runtime-$(id -u) && mkdir -p \"$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR\"\n- agent-browser crashes after extended use (memory pressure) — keep recording sessions short, restart between scenes\n- ffmpeg installed as static binary in ~/bin — add to PATH: export PATH=\"$HOME/bin:$PATH\"\n- ELEVENLABS_API_KEY not available — demo video has no voiceover\n- Demo video at .claude-beat/logs/demos/demo_2026-03-20.mp4 (5 scenes, ~1:51)\n- SQLite DB stored in `app/data/` (gitignored)\n- DB tables: users, listings, listing_members, listing_applications, user_genres, notifications, ratings, telegram_chats, pending_telegram_groups\n- Telegram bot integration requires TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN env var (see issue #21)\n- Discord bot integration requires DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN + DISCORD_CLIENT_ID + DISCORD_WEBHOOK_SECRET env vars\n- Listings have `platform_preference` field (telegram/discord)\n- Email notifications require SMTP env vars (SMTP_HOST, SMTP_PORT, SMTP_FROM, etc.)\n- nodemailer added as dependency for email sending\n- Session secret: lazy runtime check (not module-level) to avoid build failure\n- ESLint: use eslint v9 + @eslint/js v9 (not v10) to avoid peer dep conflicts\n- npm ci works cleanly (no --legacy-peer-deps needed)\n- No root/sudo access on server — can't install nginx, Docker, or system packages\n- PM2 available via npx (not globally installed)\n\n## Test Suite\n- 198 tests across 22 test files, all passing\n- Tested: auth (login, register), health, listings (browse, detail, create, join, edit, delete, telegram, discord, auto-telegram, auto-discord), notifications, profile, profile/genres, profile/reading, profile/reputation, ratings, rate-limit, telegram/setup, telegram/webhook, discord/setup, discord/webhook\n- All API routes now have test coverage\n- @vitest/coverage-v8 installed for coverage reporting\n\n## Components\n- `BookCover` — reusable book cover component with error fallback, uses `unoptimized` to bypass Next.js image proxy for Open Library covers that redirect through archive.org\n\n## Next Session Priority\n1. **URGENT**: PAT expires ~March 27 (#54) — ~3 days remaining, still needs-human\n2. Check issue #21 for Telegram token response\n3. Check production health\n4. All routes tested, 10 rounds of code review done — consider E2E testing, performance optimization, or new feature proposals\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":44,"oldLines":11,"newStart":44,"newLines":11,"lines":[" - None"," "," ## Closed Recently","+- #96: Code review round 11 — stale transaction data in approve, unmount cleanup, duplicate notifications — merged + deployed"," - #95: Code review round 10 — FK violations, stale notifications, input validation, error UX — merged + deployed"," - #93: Code review round 9 — client-side race conditions, date bugs, error handling, ARIA — merged + deployed"," - #91: Code review round 8 — timing-safe secrets, type validation, stale transaction data, email HTML escaping — merged + deployed"," - #87: Code review round 6 — logout fix, is_full reset, email subjects, webhook validation, fetch abort — merged + deployed","-- #85: Test coverage for Telegram and Discord API routes (69 new tests) — merged"," "," ## Open Issues"," - #21: Telegram bot token (needs-human) — multiple follow-up reminders sent, still waiting"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

Check issue #21 for Telegram token response\n3. Check production health\n4. All routes tested, 10 rounds of code review done — consider E2E testing, performance optimization, or new feature proposals","newString":"## Next Session Priority\n1. **URGENT**: PAT expires ~March 27 (#54) — ~3 days remaining, still needs-human\n2. Check issue #21 for Telegram token response\n3. Check production health\n4. All routes tested, 11 rounds of code review done — consider E2E testing, performance optimization, or new feature proposals","originalFile":"# Agent Memory\n\n## Project\n- **Name**: Bookmate\n- **Repo**: https://github.com/TommyBrennan/bookmate\n- **Project Board**: Bookmate (project #1 on TommyBrennan)\n- **Tech**: Next.js 15, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS v4, SQLite, iron-session\n- **Owner**: XanderZhu (human), TommyBrennan (GH account)\n\n## Current State\n- All PRD features implemented and merged (P0, P1, P2)\n- CI pipeline live: GitHub Actions runs lint + type check + build on push/PR to main\n- ESLint flat config with typescript-eslint, react-hooks, @next/eslint-plugin-next\n- Build passes clean, lint clean (no warnings)\n- Security hardening merged (PR #32, #36)\n- **Production deployed** via PM2 on port 3000 — http://89.167.127.85:3000\n- Health endpoint: `/api/health` (DB check, uptime, stats)\n- Custom 404 page (branded, with CTA)\n- OG + Twitter Card meta tags on listing pages (book cover, title, description)\n- Loading skeletons on all routes (root, listing detail, profile, notifications)\n- Error boundaries on all routes with retry functionality\n- Focus-visible styles for keyboard accessibility\n- **Accessibility improvements** (PR #49, merged + deployed):\n  - ARIA labels, roles, expanded states on all interactive elements\n  - Keyboard navigation on BookSearch dropdown (combobox pattern)\n  - autocomplete attributes on auth forms\n  - role=\"alert\" on error messages\n  - Skip-to-content link\n  - role=\"tablist\"/tab/tabpanel on profile tabs\n  - role=\"radiogroup\"/radio on format/platform buttons\n  - role=\"checkbox\" with keyboard support on approval toggle\n\n## Production\n- **URL**: http://89.167.127.85:3000\n- **Process manager**: PM2 (via npx)\n- **Config**: `app/ecosystem.config.js`\n- **Deploy script**: `scripts/deploy.sh`\n- **Logs**: `.claude-beat/logs/pm2-{out,error}.log`\n- **No reverse proxy** (nginx requires root — not available)\n- **No HTTPS** (no domain configured, no certbot)\n- PM2 process saved: `npx pm2 save` (restores on `pm2 resurrect`)\n\n## Open PRs\n- None\n\n## Closed Recently\n- #96: Code review round 11 — stale transaction data in approve, unmount cleanup, duplicate notifications — merged + deployed\n- #95: Code review round 10 — FK violations, stale notifications, input validation, error UX — merged + deployed\n- #93: Code review round 9 — client-side race conditions, date bugs, error handling, ARIA — merged + deployed\n- #91: Code review round 8 — timing-safe secrets, type validation, stale transaction data, email HTML escaping — merged + deployed\n- #87: Code review round 6 — logout fix, is_full reset, email subjects, webhook validation, fetch abort — merged + deployed\n\n## Open Issues\n- #21: Telegram bot token (needs-human) — multiple follow-up reminders sent, still waiting\n- #54: **PAT expires ~March 27** (needs-human) — GH_TOKEN renewal required, ~3 days left\n\n## Important Notes\n- GH_TOKEN loaded from `.claude-beat/.env`\n- Remote URL includes PAT: reset with `git remote set-url` if needed\n- Next.js 15 used (not 16) due to Turbopack /root permission issue\n- agent-browser WORKS if: export XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp/runtime-$(id -u) && mkdir -p \"$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR\"\n- agent-browser crashes after extended use (memory pressure) — keep recording sessions short, restart between scenes\n- ffmpeg installed as static binary in ~/bin — add to PATH: export PATH=\"$HOME/bin:$PATH\"\n- ELEVENLABS_API_KEY not available — demo video has no voiceover\n- Demo video at .claude-beat/logs/demos/demo_2026-03-20.mp4 (5 scenes, ~1:51)\n- SQLite DB stored in `app/data/` (gitignored)\n- DB tables: users, listings, listing_members, listing_applications, user_genres, notifications, ratings, telegram_chats, pending_telegram_groups\n- Telegram bot integration requires TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN env var (see issue #21)\n- Discord bot integration requires DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN + DISCORD_CLIENT_ID + DISCORD_WEBHOOK_SECRET env vars\n- Listings have `platform_preference` field (telegram/discord)\n- Email notifications require SMTP env vars (SMTP_HOST, SMTP_PORT, SMTP_FROM, etc.)\n- nodemailer added as dependency for email sending\n- Session secret: lazy runtime check (not module-level) to avoid build failure\n- ESLint: use eslint v9 + @eslint/js v9 (not v10) to avoid peer dep conflicts\n- npm ci works cleanly (no --legacy-peer-deps needed)\n- No root/sudo access on server — can't install nginx, Docker, or system packages\n- PM2 available via npx (not globally installed)\n\n## Test Suite\n- 198 tests across 22 test files, all passing\n- Tested: auth (login, register), health, listings (browse, detail, create, join, edit, delete, telegram, discord, auto-telegram, auto-discord), notifications, profile, profile/genres, profile/reading, profile/reputation, ratings, rate-limit, telegram/setup, telegram/webhook, discord/setup, discord/webhook\n- All API routes now have test coverage\n- @vitest/coverage-v8 installed for coverage reporting\n\n## Components\n- `BookCover` — reusable book cover component with error fallback, uses `unoptimized` to bypass Next.js image proxy for Open Library covers that redirect through archive.org\n\n## Next Session Priority\n1. **URGENT**: PAT expires ~March 27 (#54) — ~3 days remaining, still needs-human\n2. Check issue #21 for Telegram token response\n3. Check production health\n4. All routes tested, 10 rounds of code review done — consider E2E testing, performance optimization, or new feature proposals\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":89,"oldLines":4,"newStart":89,"newLines":4,"lines":[" 1. **URGENT**: PAT expires ~March 27 (#54) — ~3 days remaining, still needs-human"," 2. Check issue #21 for Telegram token response"," 3. Check production health","-4. All routes tested, 10 rounds of code review done — consider E2E testing, performance optimization, or new feature proposals","+4. All routes tested, 11 rounds of code review done — consider E2E testing, performance optimization, or new feature proposals"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}
